title: Operational Efficiency and Productivity Metrics
dataSources: Primary `index=business` sourcetypes defined via Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686): `dbx:erp_orders` (revenue and billable transaction counts aligned to SAP **VBRK**/lines or warehouse mirrors such as Oracle **RA_INVOICES_ALL** / NetSuite lines—cite **UC-23.8.6** when tying postings to **ACDOCA** controls); `dbx:hris_employees` or parallel `workday:worker` feeds with **UC-23.4.1** field **coalesce** (`employee_id`, `Worker_ID`, `worker_id`); `dbx:gl_opex` with cost-center joins (`cost_center_mapping.csv`). Automation telemetry lands in `index=automation` (`uipath:robot:execution`, `automation_anywhere:execution`) or bespoke `app:process_event` via Splunk HTTP Event Collector; optional ITSM overlay `snow:incident` for exception-cost narratives. Lookups: `productivity_kpi_targets.csv` bands per **kpi_name**; `process_classification.csv` for stable **process_class** buckets.
app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) for ERP/GL/HR warehouse extracts; Splunk HTTP Event Collector (built-in indexer tier configuration) for automation payloads forwarded from UiPath Orchestrator Insights/API bridges or Automation Anywhere telemetry collectors.
spl:
index=business sourcetype="dbx:erp_orders" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
| stats sum(revenue) as monthly_revenue, count as transactions
| head 1
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | eval wid=coalesce(employee_id, Worker_ID, worker_id, "")
    | eval active_flag=if(Active_Status=1 OR lower(tostring(Active_Status)) IN ("1","y","true","active"),1,0)
    | where active_flag=1 AND len(wid)>0
    | stats dc(wid) as headcount
    | head 1
  ]
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:gl_opex" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | lookup cost_center_mapping.csv cost_center OUTPUT operational_function
    | where operational_function IN ("Operations","Support","Finance Ops")
    | stats sum(amount) as opex_operations
    | head 1
  ]
| appendcols [
    search index=automation (sourcetype="uipath:robot:execution" OR sourcetype="automation_anywhere:execution" OR sourcetype="app:process_event") earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | stats sum(eval(if(automated="yes",1,0))) as auto_steps, count as total_steps
    | head 1
  ]
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="app:process_event" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | stats sum(eval(if(match(lower(tostring(status)),"fail.*"),1,0))) as failed_steps, count as monitored_steps
    | head 1
  ]
| eval revenue_per_FTE=if(headcount>0, round(monthly_revenue/headcount,0), null())
| eval cost_per_transaction=if(transactions>0 AND isnotnull(opex_operations), round(opex_operations/transactions,2), null())
| eval automation_rate=if(total_steps>0, round(100*auto_steps/total_steps,1), null())
| eval exception_rate=if(monitored_steps>0, round(100*failed_steps/monitored_steps,2), null())
| table monthly_revenue, transactions, headcount, opex_operations, revenue_per_FTE, cost_per_transaction, automation_rate, exception_rate
description: Computes multi-source operational-productivity KPIs—**revenue_per_FTE**, **cost_per_transaction** (operations operating expense divided by billable transactions), portfolio **automation_rate** with optional **process_class** splits, **exception_rate** from monitored process statuses, and order-to-cash cycle distributions via **`transaction`** spans—rolled monthly or trended across twelve periods when joined on aligned `_time` grains.
value: Chief operating officers need an internal-throughput lens finance dashboards rarely prioritize: whether staffing converts to shipped value, whether robots retired manual touches, whether failures concentrate in costly lanes—signals that precede margin expansion when automation investments hit the right workflows. Splunk stitches ERP throughput, HR denominators, GL-backed operating expense slices, and automation telemetry without waiting for quarterly spreadsheet merges; sustained swings highlight target owners before variance narratives reach investor-facing packs (**UC-23.8.1**) or balance-sheet cycle reviews (**UC-23.8.6**).
implementation: (1) Land `dbx:erp_orders`, `dbx:hris_employees`/`workday:*`, `dbx:gl_opex`, and HEC automation feeds with documented field parity to HR/finance charters; (2) publish `productivity_kpi_targets.csv`, `process_classification.csv`, and `cost_center_mapping.csv`; (3) schedule monthly operational tiles plus weekly forecasts only after FP&A agrees on partial-period logic; (4) attach alerts when automation drops materially month-over-month; (5) reconcile revenue denominators deliberately versus CFO tiles when scopes diverge.
visualization: Dashboard Studio rows: single-value KPI tiles with MoM deltas; **process_class** heatmap for automation saturation; gauge versus lookup targets; twin line charts for **revenue_per_FTE** and **cost_per_transaction** over twelve rolling fiscal periods; drill table ranking manual-heavy processes by modeled manual labor cost.

references.title: Splunk — Use the HTTP Event Collector

knownFalsePositives: Productivity optics swing when payroll-classified workers sit on unpaid leave yet remain coded active in HR snapshots—denominators inflate while revenue stays flat until staffing catches up. Badge-access feeds sometimes lag termination dates by weeks; pairing revenue-per-worker ratios with badge-headcount inadvertently celebrates phantom leverage until reconciliation closes the gap. Vendor-defined automation labels jump after reorganizing Orchestrator folders even when bots execute unchanged workloads—classification drift mimics breakthrough gains unless lookups gate taxonomy. Mergers fold acquired headcounts ahead of revenue synergies—mixing legacy enterprise resource planning scope makes efficiency ratios look hollow until finance aligns definitions across entities. Contractors billed outside HR worker IDs distort denominators when procurement staffing spikes during seasonal peaks—finance must declare contingent inclusion versus exclusion explicitly each quarter. Severance or restructuring accelerators posting through operations cost centers inflate operating-expense-per-transaction math though throughput stayed steady—finance-supplied flags belong in numerator exclusions. Idle clock minutes versus ERP-backed completions also misstate automation leverage when only vendor consoles are compared.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud per `splunkVersions`; Search macro role can read `index=business`, `index=automation`, and any optional ITSM index you route ServiceNow incidents into.
• Splunk DB Connect (`splunk_app_db_connect`, Splunkbase 2686) on a Heavy Forwarder or supported execution node with JDBC to your ERP warehouse tables (SAP **VBRK**/**VBRP**/billing facts, Oracle **RA_INVOICES_ALL**, NetSuite **transactionLine**, or an EDW star already reconciled to finance). Follow **UC-23.8.6** when shaping **ACDOCA**-style pulls so revenue denominators stay aligned with controller definitions.
• HRIS coverage using one canonical path agreed with HR: Workday **RaaS** JSON to HEC, Workday Web Services (**SOAP**) polling, or DB Connect to `hris_employees`—see **UC-23.4.1** for why no Splunkbase TA owns field casing; normalize `coalesce(employee_id, Worker_ID, worker_id, "")` before any headcount distinct count.
• Process automation telemetry via HEC (`uipath:robot:execution`, `automation_anywhere:execution`, or internal `app:process_event`) with explicit `automated`, `process_name`, `status`, and timestamps; classify stable **process_class** values through `process_classification.csv`, not raw Orchestrator labels alone.
• Operating expense slice from **GL** (`dbx:gl_opex`) with cost-center-to-function mapping (`cost_center_mapping.csv`) so operations support reorganizations without rewriting SPL quarterly.
• CSV lookups maintained under change control: `productivity_kpi_targets.csv` (bands per **kpi_name**), `process_classification.csv`, optional `cost_center_mapping.csv`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Path A — ERP revenue and billable transactions (**DB Connect**): Create a rising watermark input against your warehouse `revenue_transactions` (or ERP billing headers/lines). Example SAP-aligned SQL projects invoice date, net value in group currency, document type filters, company code, and a stable `order_id` shared with downstream **process_event** payloads. Stamp sourcetype `dbx:erp_orders`; set `_time` from `transaction_date`. Schedule after nightly ERP publish; record row counts versus **ACDOCA** revenue control totals when your charter ties operational productivity to ledger-backed sales (**UC-23.8.6** cites **VBRK**/document typing discipline).

Path B — HRIS active population (**DB Connect** snapshot preferred): JDBC poll `hris_employees` where `employment_status='Active'` (and mirror HR policy for unpaid leave and contingent worker inclusion). Emit one row per worker per poll with `employee_id`, `Worker_ID`, `worker_id`, `Active_Status`, `compensation_type`. If Workday remains authoritative, align filters with **UC-23.4.1** patterns (`Active_Status_norm`, unpaid-leave exclusions) before `dc()`—even when the warehouse snapshot is the ingest path.

Path C — Automation platforms (**HEC**): Forward UiPath Orchestrator job telemetry (Insights REST export or webhook bridge) into `index=automation` with sourcetype `uipath:robot:execution`. Minimal JSON fields: `process_name`, `robot_id`, `start_time`, `end_time`, `status`, `transaction_count`, `automated`. Example HEC POST body fragment:
{"event":{"process_name":"INV_Approval_v3","robot_id":"BOT-FIN-01","start_time":"2026-04-27T08:01:02Z","end_time":"2026-04-27T08:03:41Z","status":"Successful","transaction_count":1,"automated":"yes","sourcetype":"uipath:robot:execution"}}
Mirror equivalent fields for Automation Anywhere or bespoke BPM logs (`app:process_event`) so exception counting can fall back when ITSM overlays are absent.

Path D — Operating expense (**DB Connect**): Land `dbx:gl_opex` lines with `amount`, `cost_center`, `period`, natural account, currency. Restrict operations denominator using **finance-approved** mappings rather than raw numeric ranges alone—finance operations owns `cost_center_mapping.csv` rows keyed by `(period,cost_center)` with `operational_function`.

Path E — Support lookups: Load `productivity_kpi_targets.csv` with columns such as `kpi_name`, `target_value`, `threshold_amber`, `threshold_red`, `unit`, `owner`, `last_reviewed`. Load `process_classification.csv` mapping `process_name` → `process_class` (order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, hire-to-onboard, expense-to-reimburse). Document footer text describing canonical headcount definition and classification freshness dates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Monthly operational scorecard (single-period rollup): anchor ERP totals, then widen with defensive `appendcols` subsearches capped via `| head 1`, mirroring **UC-23.8.1**’s caution about silent widening inside appended branches.

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:erp_orders" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
| stats sum(revenue) as monthly_revenue, count as transactions
| head 1
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:hris_employees" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | eval wid=coalesce(employee_id, Worker_ID, worker_id, "")
    | eval active_flag=if(Active_Status=1 OR lower(tostring(Active_Status)) IN ("1","y","true","active"),1,0)
    | where active_flag=1 AND (len(wid)>0)
    | stats dc(wid) as headcount
    | head 1
  ]
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="dbx:gl_opex" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | lookup cost_center_mapping.csv cost_center OUTPUT operational_function
    | where operational_function IN ("Operations","Support","Finance Ops")
    | stats sum(amount) as opex_operations
    | head 1
  ]
| appendcols [
    search index=automation (sourcetype="uipath:robot:execution" OR sourcetype="automation_anywhere:execution" OR sourcetype="app:process_event") earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | stats sum(eval(if(automated="yes",1,0))) as auto_steps, count as total_steps
    | head 1
  ]
| appendcols [
    search index=business sourcetype="app:process_event" earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
    | stats sum(eval(if(match(lower(tostring(status)),"fail.*"),1,0))) as failed_steps, count as monitored_steps
    | head 1
  ]
| eval revenue_per_FTE=if(headcount>0, round(monthly_revenue/headcount,0), null())
| eval cost_per_transaction=if(transactions>0 AND isnotnull(opex_operations), round(opex_operations/transactions,2), null())
| eval automation_rate=if(total_steps>0, round(100*auto_steps/total_steps,1), null())
| eval exception_rate=if(monitored_steps>0, round(100*failed_steps/monitored_steps,2), null())
| eval kpi_name_rev="revenue_per_FTE"
| eval kpi_name_cost="cost_per_transaction"
| eval kpi_name_auto="automation_rate"
| eval kpi_name_exc="exception_rate"
```

Per-process automation breakdown (paste as sibling panel): 

```spl
index=automation (sourcetype="uipath:robot:execution" OR sourcetype="app:process_event") earliest=-1mon@mon latest=@mon
| lookup process_classification.csv process_name OUTPUT process_class
| stats sum(eval(if(automated="yes",1,0))) as auto_count, count as total_count by process_class
| eval auto_rate=round(100*auto_count/total_count, 1)
```

Order-to-cash cycle distribution (**transaction** command—not `stats` alone):

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="app:process_event" OR sourcetype="dbx:erp_orders") earliest=-120d@d latest=now
| transaction order_id startswith="OrderCreated" endswith="OrderInvoiced" maxspan=90d
| stats median(duration) as p50_oc_h, perc95(duration) as p95_oc_h
| eval p50_oc_d=round(p50_oc_h/86400, 1)
| eval p95_oc_d=round(p95_oc_h/86400, 1)
```

Twelve-month productivity trend: drive off ERP monthly buckets first (`bin _time span=1mon`), aggregate revenue and transactions per `_time`, then `join type=left _time` analogous monthly aggregates for headcount snapshots and OPEX—avoid naive `appendcols` across twelve heterogeneous grains without aligning `_time`; materializing a summary index (`summary_ops_productivity`) is the durable scale pattern once feeds stabilize.

Pipeline mechanics: ERP supplies numerator revenue and denominator transactions for throughput math; HRIS supplies active worker denominators via **coalesce** because warehouse columns vary by tenant (**UC-23.4.1**); GL supplies operations OPEX for **cost_per_transaction**—allocation policy varies; automation feeds populate **automation_rate** and feed classification overlays; optional failure telemetry yields **exception_rate**. GREEN/AMBER/RED: pivot results into rows per KPI or evaluate separate lookups (`lookup productivity_kpi_targets.csv kpi_name`) once you reshape wide metrics tall.

Scheduling: monthly cadence for executive-ready tiles; weekly intra-month projections using partial-period filters only when finance agrees on proration rules; daily alert evaluating automation_delta_month_over_month when drop exceeds five percentage points—notify chief operating officer and vice president operations distribution lists.

Step 3 — Validate
• Revenue and billable counts: reconcile with **UC-23.8.1** CFO tiles when definitions intentionally align; otherwise publish deltas (for example operational productivity counting shipped product invoices while **CEO/CFO** tile adds professional services). Tie Splunk totals to ERP billing displays (**VF03**/invoice workspace analogues per ERP family).
• Automation throughput: compare blended automation percentages against UiPath Orchestrator **Reports → Process Performance** for the same interval.
• Headcount: compare Splunk distinct worker counts with Workday **Reports → Active Workers** snapshot totals after applying identical contingent-worker exclusions.
• Cycle metrics: trace five exemplar **order_id** paths across staging tables versus Splunk **transaction** spans—median duration should track operational intuition within scheduling noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard Studio layout: Row one—single-value tiles with MoM deltas (**revenue_per_FTE**, **cost_per_transaction**, **automation_rate**, **exception_rate**). Row two—heatmap of **process_class** × month using **auto_rate**. Row three—gauge comparing portfolio **automation_rate** against annual target from lookup. Row four—table top ten manual-heavy processes ranked by `(manual_volume)*(estimated_manual_cost)`. Embed footer chips for **canonical headcount definition**, **classification review date**, and **cost-center mapping version**.

Operational rhythm: monthly chief operating officer workflow reviewing KPI movers and prioritizing automation backlog; quarterly operations committee briefing tying productivity trajectory to margin narratives for finance partners.

Ownership: accountable executive sponsor chief operating officer; day-to-day engineering owned by vice president operations with lookup stewardship split HR/finance/automation guilds.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• Headcount denominator drift across HRIS, payroll, and physical access signals phantom productivity swings—**revenue_per_FTE** can move six to fifteen percent when unpaid-leave cohorts or badge-access latency diverge from HR master data. Weekly reconciliation search example: compare distinct worker identifiers per authoritative feed before trusting denominators; mitigation locks canonical counts to Workday-active semantics excluding unpaid leave per policy (**UC-23.4.1** normalization baseline). When People teams reclassify contingent-to-employee mixes mid-quarter, rerun the reconciliation before publishing the operational pack so the denominator story matches payroll accruals.

• Automation taxonomy churn—Orchestrator folders versus UiPath Apps labels versus spreadsheet nicknames diverge mid-year; automation-rate spikes on classification edits alone. Trend `process_type` or vendor labels daily to detect step-changes unrelated to throughput; mitigation pins authoritative labels to **`process_classification.csv`** reviewed quarterly by operations strategy. Add a dashboard footnote stating the classification file version hash or `last_reviewed` so meeting-day renames cannot masquerade as efficiency wins.

• Cost-center reorganizations remap thousands of GL rows overnight—**cost_per_transaction** jumps without behavioral change when filters still reference obsolete numeric ranges. Monthly sums by **cost_center** expose discontinuities; mitigation joins **`cost_center_mapping.csv`** through `(period,cost_center)` to aggregate by **operational_function** instead of brittle literals. Pair with finance’s reorg memo date so Splunk schedules re-point filters the same week the ledger team retags spend.
grandmaExplanation: We blend sales going out the door, how many people are on staff, how much work runs without hand-holding, and how often processes fail—so leaders see if the team truly gets more done per person, not just busier days.
